post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 211.
Read everything in this post


Below the distant escarpment that marks the edge of Aria’s Retreat, a crystalline river trail reflecting restless celestials splits the steppe, giving the plain its name – the Divided Prairie. At the origin of the river, near the base of the cliff, lies the Spring of Selection. Thanks to it, glowing, vivacious rabbit-like creatures roam the grasslands, thriving in abundance.

…

It was some 700 to 800 years ago when these creatures – the Spirit Arrays – still maintained their eternal youth. However, they still faced the ever-changing threats presented by the temperamental Prairie. Those who were not prepared at any given moment perished, so the Spirits earnestly sought guidance from the land to endure.
Legends whispered across the plains hinted that the secrets to survival were within the Mirror of Insights – an eternally frozen pond, day and night, summer and winter. The clues to the singular purpose of the Mirror were the shimmering sparks it gave off during the night. These the Spirit Arrays examined to find a way to survive, and they called them Insights.
Insights gave the Spirits’ creativity to resolve the menaces of nature. The Spirits utilized the Mirror throughout what became known as the Immortality Era, which preceded the Catalyst Era. Creativity enabled them to maintain their numbers for a while, but soon the metamorphosies of nature superannuated the Insights on the surface of the Mirror that they were able to study. Thus the Spirits, unable to keep up with the capricious Prairie, once again waned one after another.
The surviving remnants traced back the legends and gathered at the Mirror of Insights -- their last hope of preserving their kind. They prayed fervently to the stars for answers, answers before the light of day appeared for one last time – for when morning arrived the few that remained would succumb to nature’s mercurial retribution.
As the luminaries faded and the skies were unveiled, the solar radiance must have heard their invocations, for warmth rained down and dissolved the frost of the Mirror. Water erupted from below the ice, shattering the frigid placidity. That water, which was the first ever to be seen by the spirits, channeled their life forces into a new entity – inheritance. Embracing it, the Spirits sacrificed their eternal youth to become the origins of their ancestry. The source of the water, what the Mirror of Insight manifested itself to be, was named the Spring of Selection; its water, nourishing a river which was called the Stream of Proliferation. Thus began the Catalyst Era.

…

Inheritance and selection allowed the Spirit Arrays to flourish throughout the generations. However, as the sun set for the night, the Spring of Selection would freeze, and the Stream of Proliferation would crystallize. Like the Mirror, shimmering sparks still provided Insights of creativity as the water was frozen. Now blessed with their new carefree lifestyle, unconcerned with survival, the Spirits could delve through these Insights for creativity to pursue their personal desires. It was agreed that, upon daybreak, every kindred would conjure the solar radiance together to dissolve the frosted Spring to restore the river again. Thus they would propagate by day, and fulfill their wishes by night.



Basic Rules
1. Do not post at night.
2. Do not talk about this game anywhere else, unless it's to me or the co-moderator or if we explicitly allow you to do so.
3. Do not conceal text in any way, such as making your text white or making it excessively small. (Hide tags are allowed)
4. Do not edit or delete posts for any reason.
5. Do not post if you are dead or not in the game.


Voting
1. Vote in the form of Vote: <insert exact player name without brackets> or Vote: No Hang. Unvote in the form of Unvote. Correct capitalization is needed for a vote or unvote to count.
2. Votes in hide tags will be ignored.
3. No Hang is achieved at parity; Majority Hang is achieved at majority.
4. Majority Hang is enabled starting Day 2; Deadline Hang is always in effect.
5. In the case of a tie at the end of a day, a person from those with the most votes will be randomly hanged.
6. Votes are locked when # of town - 1 = # of mafia.
7. Don't try to fakevote. Anything that looks like a real vote will be counted.


Phases and Activity
1. Phases are 11/3.
2. If you do not make at least 10 game-advancing posts during each day phase, you will be replaced.
3. PM prods will be sent at the mod/co-mod’s discretion.
4. V/LA’s should be bolded.


Game Related Rules and Etiquette
1. Follow AoPS community rules and guidelines.
2. Respect other players.
3. Do not ask any game-related or mechanics questions in this thread. Ask them in your role PM.
4. Do not quote or pretend to quote anything in your role PM or anything the mod/co-mod said to you privately.
5. Use common sense and have a mental age of more than 13.


Mentor Game-Specific Rules
1. Do not address other players' mentors in any circumstances. This includes mentioning them at all.
2. Do not quote or pretend to quote anything your mentor said to you privately.
3. Mentors can talk to their mentees in their own quicktopics (or Discord private DM if requested). Mafia mentors can talk in the factional chat.
4. Your mentor isn't playing the game for you. If you've read everything in this post, send me a summary of your role in your role PM.


Moderator Information
1. HannahW and Fvr_Vain are the co-moderator and moderator of this game, respectively.
2. You can request activity checks and votecounts in the thread or in your role PM with something like !ac or !vc.
3. Rule infractions will result in warnings, force-replacements, or modkills, depending on severity of the infraction, which will be determined at the discretion of HannahW and I. I don't want to go there so try not to break anything.
4. Don’t lie to the mod/co-mod.
5. Don’t use these colors (or anything similar): #38761d, #f00, #00f.
6. Abide by everything we tell you in this game.


Setup
This game will be using the Vigilante 10er setup. This is an open setup:

7 Vanilla Town
1 Town Vigilante
2 Mafia Goons

- Mafia have a daychat.
- Kills are indistinguishable.
- Factional kills are mandatory.

Role PM's
(Adapted from MafiaScum wiki)
Vanilla Town wrote:
Welcome, [Player Name], you are a Vanilla Town.

You have no active abilities.

You win if all threats to the town are eliminated and at least one town-aligned player is alive.

Confirm by reading all the rules and finding the confirm rule.
Town Vigilante wrote:
Welcome, [Player Name], you are a Town Vigilante.

Vigilante Kill: Each night phase, you may kill another player.

You win if all threats to the town are eliminated and at least one town-aligned player is alive.

Confirm by reading all the rules and finding the confirm rule.
Mafia Goon wrote:
Welcome, [Player Name]. You are a Mafia Goon, along with your partner, [Player Name].

Factional Communication: You may talk to your group anytime here [Link].
Factional Kill: Each night phase, you must collectively kill a player.

You win when all town are dead and at least one Mafia-member is alive, or if nothing can prevent this from happening.

Confirm by reading all the rules and finding the confirm rule.
There are no flavor texts in role PM’s.


Alive
The following players (mentors) are alive:

avanders sheepsaysmeep (fdas)
xzhang VKU247 (summitwei)
BobDBuilder321 (chess24)
Mathletesv (sudzoof)
mathjay mark888 (e_is_cool)
Madelyn (tikkitamatoa)
bigmath (smallpeoples343)

Dead
The following players (mentors) are dead:

kevinmathz (atomicpianowitch) - Vanilla Town
midwaybear (aroodman) - Town Vigilante
snoozingnewt (NDMath) - Vanilla Town

Events
Day 1 Hang - kevinmathz was Vanilla Town*
Night 1 Deaths - midwaybear was Town Vigilante, snoozingnewt was Vanilla Town*


Warnings
kevinmathz receives a warning for unnecessary spam.*
midwaybear receives a warning for discussion of mentors*
mathjay is force replaced for out of thread discussion*
Mathletesv receives a warning for addressing mentors*


Day 2 deadline is on Thursday, September 10th, at 10:00 AM EDT.

post_rendered:
The descendants of the Spirit Arrays formed ten ancestries, each originating from one of the fated spirits who survived the Immortality Era. Propagating through the past seven centuries, each of these ancestries associated into a distinct kindred probing for their own Insights. Despite their differences in creativity and culture, they still together ensured the Stream of Proliferation's flux upon each daybreak.

---

One morning, as the Spirit Arrays gathered as usual before the Spring of Selection, again invoking the sun's warmth, the Spring didn’t dissolve. This time, it wasn't enough for the Stream of Proliferation to run again.

Over the years, they had learned that removing the permafrost from the night before required the power of all who resided on the Divided Prairie. As the icy Spring scattered the morning sunlight, It became clear that some of them now wished to prioritize their own insights over their collective survival. The ten kindreds, becoming suspicious of each other, recognized that they needed to eliminate the ancestries that now threatened their permanence.

Echoing their predecessors, they sought guidance from the Prairie. The visionaries of each kindred discovered that Insights delved by some of them granted the power to manipulate natural contingencies to select against designated traits at will. Aghast by this discovery, they searched for ways to retaliate from their collective records, the Chronicles of Evolution.

Prophecies suggested that by now, some spirits who are devoted to inheritance have evolved the power of selection themselves, able to purify and bring judgment to any who they deem tainted by the avarice of Insight. They can only hope that the prophecies' subjects can bring harmony back to the Divided Prairie again


Fvr_Vain was frozen, he was the Spring of Selection.
HannahW was crystallized, she was the Stream of Proliferation.

One morning, as the Spirit Arrays gathered as usual before the Spring of Selection, again invoking the sun's warmth, the Spring didn’t dissolve. This time, it wasn't enough for the Stream of Proliferation to run again.

mafiascum wiki wrote:
Confirmation Bias or Tunnel Vision is when a player becomes convinced by their own arguments by virtue of how long or how strong they hold them. It is a form of Logical Fallacy, much like Appeal to Probability but applying to the person who makes the argument, not their audience.

It is not always a false assumption, but it can be blinding to new or better options that come along, because they do not match the player's "pet theory".

post_rendered:
Here's a good plan to do:

Hang someone day 1. Then we split the remaining players into 3 groups according to player order in the OP. Group 1 will be 1-3, group 2 will be 4-6, group 3 will be 7-9.

If you're vig, then vig someone in one of the other two groups. Then, we kill off the rest of the people in that group via hanging and vigging. As long as at least one person in that group is scum, then we are in a good place since we will at least make it to f3. If two people in that group are scum it is gg since we win. There is a 1 in 28 chance that vig and both scum are in the same group, which means that as long as the vig is pretty good at scumhunting, then we will take down at least one scum.

I think this is an optimal strategy to play due to the fact that unless the 1/28 chance that vig and both scum are in the same groupoccurs or vig is really bad at scumhunting, then scum has to choose between 4-5 people to try to get out the vig for night 2, so vig is likely to survive 3-4 nights and would likely not have to claim.

so
run this by your mentors but basically
tunneling is when you focus on a read to the detriment and exclusion of absolutelypretty much/kinda/mostly everything else that is happening in the game
ascribe varying degrees of severity here etc etc etc
motivation reads are when you consider the respective town and scum motivations behind someone's actions and/or posts and then determine one to be greater than the other
the last part is the important part because everyone pretty much does it automatically when theyre making motivation reads
but somehow forgets it when theyre critiquing motivation reads
and the reason i am going over this very very basic concept (again) is because bad motivation reads =/= tunneling
bad motivation reads being when u disagree with someone's motivation reads and think they sr'd something that's kinda nai or maybe even the opposite
and the reason why that is important is because the way you deal with tunneling is to stop it. the way you deal with bad motivation reads is tell people to look at motivations again and reevaluate whether they still think town/scum motivation is greater than the other and or throw various arguments at them to change their mind
you cannot misapply this
you. cannot. mix and match this.
tl;dr do not call possibly bad/questionable motivation reads tunneling and tell people to drop those attacks
instead tell them why you disagree with their interpretation of those motives etc etc etc

tunneling is when you focus on a read to the detriment and exclusion of absolutelypretty much/kinda/mostly everything else that is happening in the game
ascribe varying degrees of severity here etc etc etc
motivation reads are when you consider the respective town and scum motivations behind someone's actions and/or posts [i]and then determine one to be greater than the other[/i]
the last part is the important part because everyone pretty much does it automatically when theyre making motivation reads
but somehow forgets it when theyre critiquing motivation reads
and the reason i am going over this very very basic concept (again) is because bad motivation reads =/= tunneling 
bad motivation reads being when u disagree with someone's motivation reads and think they sr'd something that's kinda nai or maybe even the opposite
and the reason why that is important is because the way you deal with tunneling is to stop it. the way you deal with bad motivation reads is tell people to look at motivations again and reevaluate whether they still think town/scum motivation is greater than the other and or throw various arguments at them to change their mind
you cannot misapply this
you. cannot. mix and match this. 
tl;dr do not call possibly bad/questionable motivation reads tunneling and tell people to drop those attacks

Here's a good plan to do:

Hang someone day 1. Then we split the remaining players into 3 groups according to player order in the OP. Group 1 will be 1-3, group 2 will be 4-6, group 3 will be 7-9.

If you're vig, then vig someone in one of the other two groups. Then, we kill off the rest of the people in that group via hanging and vigging. As long as at least one person in that group is scum, then we are in a good place since we will at least make it to f3. If two people in that group are scum it is gg since we win. There is a 1 in 28 chance that vig and both scum are in the same group, which means that as long as the vig is pretty good at scumhunting, then we will take down at least one scum.

I think this is an optimal strategy to play due to the fact that unless the 1/28 chance that vig and both scum are in the same groupoccurs or vig is really bad at scumhunting, then scum has to choose between 4-5 people to try to get out the vig for night 2, so vig is likely to survive 3-4 nights and would likely not have to claim.

kevinmathz wrote:
Here's a good plan to do:

Hang someone day 1. Then we split the remaining players into 3 groups according to player order in the OP. Group 1 will be 1-3, group 2 will be 4-6, group 3 will be 7-9.

If you're vig, then vig someone in one of the other two groups. Then, we kill off the rest of the people in that group via hanging and vigging. As long as at least one person in that group is scum, then we are in a good place since we will at least make it to f3. If two people in that group are scum it is gg since we win. There is a 1 in 28 chance that vig and both scum are in the same group, which means that as long as the vig is pretty good at scumhunting, then we will take down at least one scum.

I think this is an optimal strategy to play due to the fact that unless the 1/28 chance that vig and both scum are in the same groupoccurs or vig is really bad at scumhunting, then scum has to choose between 4-5 people to try to get out the vig for night 2, so vig is likely to survive 3-4 nights and would likely not have to claim.

One of xzhang's posts in 206 states a lack of time, which could be why he sheeped in 199, but didn't in 206. So I don't think xzhang sheeping is scummy for him.

In 206 and 199, xzhang didn't take many stances, instead posted observations like he did this game. So I don't think we can find him scummy for that.

I want to apologize for some of the modding complications on day 1.
I was postbanned for toxicity, if that caused any psychological harm I was not considering consequences. I hope that didn’t impact the game too much. Thanks to Hannah everything went fine.

mathjay was replaced for talking out of thread and continuing to OOT when I told him not to. It didn’t have to do with whatever else that happened.

Start of school got in the way of other things. I have a lot of classes and a couple of really important contests this year that I have to stay on top of. OP wasn’t really updated but I’m glad the game ended without too many problems.

Some issues that I sort of observed from mentor/mentee chats:

- Mentors directly influencing reads.
- Mentees overdoing what mentors telling them to do
- Mentees completely forgets what mentors told them.

Rule issues that I probably should have considered:

- Angleshooting
- Mentors should try not give reads or agree/disagree with mentee reads

post_rendered:
ok i just went over what was said in wolfchat

and i'll say it here so more people can learn from this

first, there's no such thing as pr hunting. all pr tells are unreliable because they can be faked.

however, there is vt hunting. it's much harder to fake a vt tell.

one of the biggest vt tells is of course being top wagon near eod and not claiming. there are obviously exceptions to this (like me) but in general this is true. it really depends on the player though

a few people in this game, namely bob and snoozing, gave themselves a decent amount of vt equity by talking about wanting to be shot. this sort of mentality is just hard to see coming from a vig, who isn't really thinking about who the vig will shoot, but who they themselves will shoot, and of course they would exclude themselves. this is relatively easy to fake though, but it is something to note if someone requests a pr to target them.

and, one tell i actually found during the game was from mathlete. near eod, he talked about vig actions, which is fine and all. however, he insisted that the vig shoot the counterwagon, and repeated it constantly. this made it clear that mathlete was not likely vig, but instead was the vt trying to make sure the vig listened to them.

something of note that is probably confirmation bias but near eod1 midway posted a reads list with the statement of "assuming vig follows our plan" which could be seen as tmi on what the vig would do.

of course these style of tells arent always accurate but it gives you a good idea of what to look for and i hope this makes up for my complete lack of motivation following mathjay getting replacing out
